prerequisites: A Google Cloud project with the Cloud Storage API available and billing enabled.
An existing Cloud Storage bucket to write to (provide its name in gcs_bucket_name) and a path prefix within it (gcs_bucket_path).
Knowledge of the bucket's location if you want to set gcs_bucket_region. A location can be a region (for example US-CENTRAL1), a dual-region, or a multi-region (for example US).
A service account in the project to associate the HMAC key with. HMAC keys are a distinct credential type from the normal RSA service account keys.

IAM permissions prefixed with storage.hmacKeys on the project to create and manage the key (for example the Storage HMAC Key Admin role), plus the org policy constraint constraints/storage.restrictAuthTypes disabled so it does not block HMAC authentication.
A decision on the output file format to write (the required format config field).

authMethods.name: HMAC key (Access ID and Secret)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate to Cloud Storage using an HMAC key, the credential type that maps to the connector's credential.hmac_key_access_id and credential.hmac_key_secret secret fields. An HMAC key consists of an Access ID (an alphanumeric string linked to a specific account, 61 characters when linked to a service account) and a Secret (a 40-character Base-64 encoded string linked to that Access ID). HMAC keys are used to create signatures that authorize requests to the Cloud Storage XML API for interoperability. The secret is given only once at creation and must be stored securely, because a lost secret cannot be retrieved by you or Google Cloud and you must create a new key.

setupSteps.title: Create or choose a bucket
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud project, create a Cloud Storage bucket or pick an existing one. Record its name for gcs_bucket_name and decide on a path prefix for gcs_bucket_path. A location is chosen for the bucket (a region, dual-region, or multi-region); note that location if you intend to populate gcs_bucket_region.

setupSteps.title: Prepare a service account and HMAC permissions
setupSteps.body: Ensure a service account exists in the project and that you hold IAM permissions prefixed with storage.hmacKeys (for example the Storage HMAC Key Admin role). Confirm the org policy constraint constraints/storage.restrictAuthTypes is disabled so it does not block HMAC authentication.

setupSteps.title: Create an HMAC key for the service account
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud console, go to the Cloud Storage Settings page, select the Interoperability tab, click 'Create a key for a service account', select the target service account, and click 'Create key'. You can also run gcloud storage hmac create SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL. Copy the displayed Access ID and Secret immediately, because the secret is only shown once.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials and bucket configuration
setupSteps.body: Enter the Access ID into credential.hmac_key_access_id and the Secret into credential.hmac_key_secret. Set gcs_bucket_name, gcs_bucket_path, and optionally gcs_bucket_region, then choose the output format. Verify the service account has write access to the bucket per Cloud Storage access control.

sources.title: Manage HMAC keys for service accounts | Cloud Storage
